---
title: "In-Kyun Kim"
subtitle: "HCMC Fellow, June E. Huh Center for Mathematical Challenges"
---

::: {.profile-box}
**Email:** [soulcraw@kias.re.kr](mailto:soulcraw@kias.re.kr)  
**Affiliation:** June E. Huh Center for Mathematical Challenges, Korea Institute for Advanced Study
:::

## Who am I?

I am a HCMC Fellow at the June E. Huh Center for Mathematical Challenges, Korea Institute for Advanced Study. My primary research interests are in algebraic geometry and complex geometry, particularly birational geometry. More precisely, my current research focuses on the study of Fano varieties and their applications.

## Research overview

My research is in algebraic geometry, with a particular focus on birational geometry, Fano varieties, K-stability, and singularity theory.

A central theme of my work is the geometry of weighted projective varieties. In particular, I study the birational and metric properties of weighted Fano hypersurfaces, including questions related to K-stability, K-moduli spaces, delta-invariants, and cylinders.

I am also interested in singularities and their Hodge-theoretic invariants, including log canonical thresholds, Hodge ideals, Du Bois singularities, Bernstein–Sato polynomials, and the spectra of hypersurface singularities.

## Research interests

- Algebraic geometry
- Birational geometry
- Fano varieties
- Weighted projective varieties
- Log canonical thresholds
- K-stability and K-moduli
- Kähler–Einstein metrics
- Cylinders and affine geometry
- Mixed Hodge modules

## Selected recent work

- Wall-crossing for K-moduli spaces of certain families of weighted projective hypersurfaces.
- Cylinders in weighted Fano varieties.

See the full list on the [Research](research.qmd) page.

## Contact information

June E. Huh Center for Mathematical Challenges, Korea Institute for Advanced Study  
85 Hoegiro, Dongdaemun-gu, Seoul 02455, Republic of Korea

Email: [soulcraw@kias.re.kr](mailto:soulcraw@kias.re.kr)
